result.summary: Firebase exposes HTTP APIs for Management, Authentication, Cloud Messaging, App Distribution, and Realtime Database Management plus the Firebase CLI and an MCP server; authenticate with either a Firebase Web API key for the Auth REST API or Google OAuth 2.0 credentials/ADC (including `firebase login` for CLI/MCP and Bearer tokens for the other APIs).

firebase_web_api_key.label: Firebase Web API key
firebase_web_api_key.setup: In the [Firebase console](https://console.firebase.google.com/), open your project and use the project settings / app configuration to retrieve the Firebase Web API key. Firebase documents that Firebase automatically creates API keys for projects and explains how to view and manage them in [Learn about using and managing API keys for Firebase](https://firebase.google.com/docs/projects/api-keys). Use the key as the `key` query parameter for Identity Toolkit / Secure Token REST calls.

google_oauth_access_token.label: Google OAuth 2.0 access token for your Firebase/Google Cloud project
google_oauth_access_token.setup: Create Google Cloud credentials in the [Google Cloud console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) or use Application Default Credentials as described by the [Firebase CLI reference](https://firebase.google.com/docs/cli). For user-based local auth, the easiest path is `firebase login`; for headless environments, Firebase recommends Application Default Credentials. Use the resulting OAuth 2.0 access token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` when calling Google-managed Firebase APIs.
